name: Glazed Raspberry Fudge Cake
recipeIngredient: 5 oz Semisweet chocolate; melt and cool slightly
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together cake flour and baking powder.
2. In a large bowl, cream the butter and 3/4 cup of sugar until light and fluffy.
3. Add egg yolks one at a time, mixing well after each addition.
4. Stir in melted chocolate, cocoa, vanilla extract, rum, and water; mix until combined.
5. Sift together flour mixture over the wet ingredients and fold gently to combine.
6. In another large, grease-free bowl, beat egg whites on low speed with cream of tartar and salt until soft peaks form.
7. Gradually add remaining sugar while continuing to beat until soft, but still slightly stiff peaks remain.
8. Gently stir in 1/4 of the white mixture into the batter.
9. Fold in the remaining egg whites using a spatula or whisk.
10. Pour batter into a greased (9-inch) springform pan with a parchment or wax paper base and smooth the top.
11. Bake at 350°F until well browned, crusty, and a wooden pick inserted into the center comes out without touching any batter (it should not be dry), about 35 minutes.
12. Cool in the pan on a cooling rack until completely cooled; the cake will deflate as it cools.
13. Carefully loosen the edges of the cake using a flexible knife, then gently turn it out onto a serving platter with the parchment side facing up.
14. To make the glaze, heat raspberry preserves until flowing and brush over the top and sides of the cooled cake.
15. In a microwave-safe or small pot dish, melt butter and chocolate together.
16. Add powdered sugar and rum to the melted mixture; stir well until smooth.
17. Let the glaze stand until it thickens slightly but remains spreadable.
18. Spread the glaze over the cake using a flexible spatula.
19. Serve with raspberries passed separately in a bowl.
Note: The recipe can be made ahead of time and held at room temperature, covered airtight.
